domain: Welsh cynghanedd (cross-harmony bardic poetic constraint) — ordered consonant mirroring across cæsura within a line; obligatory hard constraint in 24 strict-metre Welsh forms; Cynghanedd Groes is strict mirror; codified Caerwys 1523.
specific_mechanism: Hard ordered consonant-class correspondence between two halves of a line, plus internal rhyme between accented syllables; sub-types (Groes strict, Draws partial, Sain hybrid, Lusg echo) define different degrees of strictness.
llm_application: CYNGHANEDD-MIRROR consonant-class symmetry pretrain: auxiliary pretraining loss L_cyn that, on paired sentences (A=paraphrase of B, or A↔B parallel construction), enforces ordered class-sequence correspondence between the learnable K-class codebook IDs of tokens in A and tokens in B. Specifically: (1) learn a K=32 class codebook over token embeddings via VQ; (2) for paired (A, B) in pre-training, compute cluster_id(a_i) and cluster_id(b_i); (3) L_cyn = sum_i 1[cluster_id(a_i) != cluster_id(b_i)] minimized as auxiliary loss term added to NTP. Differs from (a) contrastive pair loss (full embedding similarity not class sequence), (b) BERT span-masking (no symmetry), (c) Beyond-Multi-Token-Prediction (future-summary not symmetric pair), (d) VQ-VAE (no pair constraint). Recombines K-class VQ codebook + paired-sentence pretraining + ordered class-sequence match.
novelty_claim: VQ-VAE provides discrete codebook over embeddings. T5 paraphrase pretraining provides paired sentence corpus. Contrastive learning provides paired-sentence loss. CYNGHANEDD-MIRROR recombines (a) K-CLASS DISCRETE CODEBOOK + (b) PAIRED-SENTENCE PRETRAIN CORPUS + (c) ORDERED POSITION-BY-POSITION CLASS-SEQUENCE MATCH as conjunction auxiliary loss. Three known components combined; not wholly new.
